title: Amira's Big Helper Day
synopsis: When Amira's little brother gets sick, she discovers that being a big sister helper can be both challenging and rewarding, especially with help from her diverse group of friends.

pages.text: Amira loved Saturday mornings. She could play with her toys. She could read her books. But today was different. Her little brother Omar was sick. He had a fever and felt very tired.

illustration.description: A cozy bedroom scene with Amira, a young girl with curly black hair and warm brown skin, sitting on her bed looking concerned. Her little brother Omar lies in a nearby bed with a thermometer, looking pale but peaceful. Sunlight streams through colorful curtains, creating a warm, caring atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Amira sitting on her bed looking at her sick brother Omar

pages.text: "Mama, can I help?" asked Amira. Mama smiled. "Yes, you can be my big helper today." Amira felt proud. She was going to take care of Omar!

illustration.description: Amira standing tall with her hands on her hips, beaming with pride. Her mama, a woman with hijab and kind eyes, smiles warmly at her daughter. The kitchen background shows morning light and family photos on the walls, creating a loving family atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Amira looking proud while talking to her mama in the kitchen

pages.text: Amira brought Omar some water. She spilled it on the floor. She tried to make him soup. The soup was too salty. "This is harder than I thought," said Amira.

illustration.description: Amira in the kitchen looking frustrated with soup splattered around and a puddle of water on the floor. Her expression shows determination mixed with worry. The kitchen has warm yellow walls and colorful dish towels, maintaining a cheerful despite the mess.
illustration.alt: Amira standing in a messy kitchen after trying to help

pages.text: Omar started to cry. His nose was stuffy. His head hurt. Amira didn't know what to do. "I'm not a good helper," she said sadly.

illustration.description: Amira sitting next to Omar's bed looking dejected with her head in her hands. Omar is crying softly with tissues nearby. The room has soft purple and blue tones, showing the sadness of the moment but still maintaining hope through gentle lighting.
illustration.alt: Amira looking sad while Omar cries in his bed

pages.text: Ding dong! The doorbell rang. It was Amira's friend Luis. "I brought coloring books!" he said. Next came Zara with her dad. "We made cookies!" said Zara.

illustration.description: Amira opening the front door to see Luis, a boy with short brown hair and a bright smile, holding colorful books. Behind him stands Zara, a girl with beautiful dark skin and braided hair, holding a plate of cookies with her tall dad smiling warmly. The doorway frames this happy scene.
illustration.alt: Amira's friends Luis and Zara arriving at the door with gifts

pages.text: "We heard Omar was sick," said Luis. "We want to help too!" said Zara. Amira felt so happy. Her friends cared about her family.

illustration.description: The three children standing together in the living room, all smiling and looking determined to help. The room has cozy furniture with bright cushions and family photos, creating a warm community feeling with rich oranges and blues.
illustration.alt: Amira, Luis, and Zara standing together ready to help

pages.text: Luis read funny stories to Omar. Zara helped clean up the water mess. Amira gave Omar the yummy cookies. Working together was much better!

illustration.description: A bustling scene with Luis sitting by Omar's bed reading animatedly with big gestures, Zara mopping the floor with a smile, and Amira feeding Omar a cookie. Everyone looks happy and busy helping, with warm golden light filling the room.
illustration.alt: The children all working together to help Omar feel better

pages.text: Omar giggled at Luis's silly voices. He ate two cookies. "I feel a little better," Omar said with a small smile. Amira hugged her friends. "Thank you for helping!"

illustration.description: Omar sitting up in bed with a gentle smile, looking much better. Amira is in the middle of a group hug with Luis and Zara, all beaming with joy. Soft sunlight creates a magical, heartwarming glow around the children, emphasizing their friendship.
illustration.alt: Omar smiling in bed while Amira hugs her friends

pages.text: Later, Mama tucked Omar into bed. "How do you feel?" asked Amira. "Much better, thanks to my big sister," said Omar. Amira smiled big.

illustration.description: A peaceful bedtime scene with Mama, wearing a soft purple hijab, gently tucking Omar into bed while Amira stands nearby glowing with pride. The room is softly lit with a nightlight, creating a serene, loving family moment with warm purples and golds.
illustration.alt: Mama tucking Omar into bed while Amira watches proudly

pages.text: "Being a helper is easier with friends," Amira told Mama. "Yes," said Mama. "And you are the best big sister helper of all." Amira felt proud and happy.

illustration.description: Amira and Mama sitting together on the couch, both smiling warmly at each other. Through the window, the evening sky shows soft pinks and purples. The living room glows with cozy lamp light, showing family photos and the lingering signs of the day's helpful activities.
illustration.alt: Amira and Mama having a loving conversation on the couch

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: Why was Omar staying in bed?
choices: He was sick with a fever
questions.explanation: Omar was sick with a fever, which made him feel tired and need to rest in bed.

questions.question: What happened when Amira tried to help by herself?
choices: She spilled water and made salty soup
questions.explanation: Amira had trouble helping alone - she spilled water on the floor and made soup that was too salty.

questions.question: What did Luis bring to help?
questions.explanation: Luis brought coloring books to help entertain Omar while he was sick.

questions.question: How did working with friends make Amira feel?
questions.explanation: Amira felt happy when her friends came to help, and she realized working together was much better.

questions.question: What did Amira learn about being a helper?
questions.explanation: Amira learned that being a helper is easier when you work together with friends.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words
pages.text: Amira wanted to _____ her little brother Omar when he was _____. At first, helping was _____ to do alone. But when her _____ Luis and Zara came over, working _____ made everything better!

pages.title: Complete the Story
pages.text: Luis brought _____ books to read to Omar. Zara helped _____ up the water mess. Omar felt _____ after eating the yummy cookies and hearing funny stories.
